mechanism_narrative: Steady expansion in real GDP reflects aggregate demand growth, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The trend signal is always active and does not depend on cross-sectional thresholds.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or the sign correlation with 60-day returns is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate by 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market weakness that depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the average excess return over baseline is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a broad weakening of consumer and investment demand, which typically precipitates lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month
failure_condition: More than 50% of joint-decline months are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound (0.1%), a subsequent increase above 0.25% is interpreted as the start of a tightening cycle, which raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After the trigger, 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of cases, or the average return is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing activity and aggregate supply, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns. The signal is always on without requiring any threshold condition.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between INDPRO growth and 20-day SPX returns is negative or below 0.10 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the consumer price index (above 0.3%) signals rising inflation that may prompt the central bank to tighten policy, increasing uncertainty and discount rates, thereby causing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: More than 50% of triggered events are followed by positive 5-day returns, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent contraction in payroll employment and a rise in the unemployment rate constitute a clear signal of labor market distress, which erodes consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: In more than 50% of joint-signal months, 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive, or the average return is not negative and statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts often peak before a broader economic slowdown. A decline of more than 5% from a three-month high in housing starts signals a peak in the housing cycle, which historically precedes a downturn in equities over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Less than 60% of such declines are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the average return is not significantly different from zero.
